sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Elche are in poor form, having lost their last three matches and failing to win in their last seven games. Osasuna, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last four matches, securing two wins and two draws, showing greater confidence and momentum heading into this clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, matches between Elche and Osasuna have been tight, with one draw (1-1 in September 2025) and an Osasuna victory (2-1 in April 2023). Historically, Osasuna have the edge, remaining unbeaten in their last 10 head-to-heads, with both teams often scoring in recent meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Elche, sitting 15th with 24 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 35 goals this season (15th worst in the league), despite a decent attacking output of 31 goals. Osasuna, 9th with 29 points, have a balanced record with 28 goals scored and conceded, bolstered by key performer Ante Budimir (11 goals), which could exploit Elche's leaky defense.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Elche, Álvaro Rodriguez (5 goals, 4 assists) will be crucial in breaking through Osasuna's defense. Osasuna's Ante Budimir, in fine form with 5 goals in his last 5 matches, poses a significant threat upfront and could be the difference-maker.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Elche's high possession (58.3%, 3rd in the league) contrasts with Osasuna's more direct approach, often capitalizing on late goals (35.7% of goals in the 76-90 minute range). Elche must shore up their defense against Osasuna's knack for scoring in key moments, while Osasuna may look to exploit Elche's vulnerability in aerial duels (18th in league).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Osasuna's better form and historical dominance in this fixture, they hold a slight edge, though Elche's home advantage and attacking potential suggest a competitive match. Sofascore's prediction (39% home win, 28% draw, 33% away win) aligns with a tight contest, but Osasuna's momentum might just tip the scales.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Elche and Osasuna are locked in a goalless draw at Estadio Martínez Valero. Neither side has managed to find the back of the net despite some promising moments. Elche has dominated possession and shown more intent in the final third, creating a notable opportunity but failing to convert. Osasuna, on the other hand, has been more reserved, focusing on defensive solidity while occasionally threatening on the counter. Both teams have displayed discipline, with only one yellow card apiece, setting the stage for a potentially more open second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Elche's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Elche has controlled much of the game, enjoying a significant share of the ball and frequently entering the opposition's penalty area. They’ve created a big chance but couldn’t capitalize, with a key miss proving costly so far. Their defensive structure has been steady, limiting Osasuna’s attacking forays, while midfielders have worked hard to maintain pressure. However, their inability to turn dominance into goals remains a concern as they head into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Osasuna's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna has adopted a more cautious approach, ceding possession to Elche while focusing on compact defending. They’ve had fewer entries into the attacking third and struggled to create clear-cut chances, with their efforts mostly limited to sporadic counter-attacks. Their aerial dominance and duel-winning ability have helped them stay in the game, but they’ve lacked the cutting edge needed to trouble Elche’s defense consistently. A more proactive approach may be required after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Elche largely dictating the attacking tempo, with consistent spells of pressure in the early stages and several sustained periods of dominance throughout. They maintained momentum for long stretches, frequently pushing forward with intent. Osasuna had their moments, particularly with brief bursts around the midway point of the half, but their attacking phases were far less frequent and often fizzled out quickly. The momentum clearly favored Elche, though they couldn’t translate it into a breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Elche, Álvaro Rodriguez has been a focal point in attack, showing promise with dangerous positioning and involvement in key moments, despite missing a big chance. His ability to find space could be crucial in the second half. On the Osasuna side, Ante Budimir remains a potential game-changer up front, even if he’s been quiet so far. His physical presence could exploit any defensive lapses from Elche if given better service after the interval.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Elche continue their push for a goal, capitalizing on their first-half dominance and home advantage. They’ll need to be more clinical in front of goal to break the deadlock. Osasuna, meanwhile, may look to exploit counter-attacking opportunities as Elche commits more players forward. Tactical adjustments could see Osasuna take more risks, but their defensive resilience will be tested. A tight contest is expected, with Elche holding a slight edge to find the opener.
